meta.title: NAAC — Intent Redelivery Storm: Full Fix Proposal
meta.description: Three layered bugs that turned one downed FMM daemon into 1,373 duplicate intent records for a single chain. Tier 1 ship-now defenses + Tier 2 correctness pass.

props.title: The 1,373-Record Bug
props.subtitle: How one downed FMM daemon turned at-least-once delivery into a full-disk attack on the recorder.

props.content: We hunted a missing readback. We found a hot loop. **Three independent bugs** — a recorder that appends JSONL into a `.json` file, a JetStream subscriber that NAKs without backoff or a delivery cap, and an intent handler that crashes when FMM is down — compose into a redelivery storm that writes ~7 records per second per stuck chain. This is the proposal to fix all three, in two tiers.

props.label: DIAGNOSIS
props.title: What we observed in 90 seconds
props.subtitle: The PTT pane went silent. The diff pane errored on startup. The server pane reported `fmm-daemon: exited with code 1`. Then we opened the recorder.

props.content:
**Surface symptom:** scenario_diff died with `Expecting property name enclosed in double quotes: line 429 column 1` when loading `end_to_end_complete_flight.json`. fmm-daemon died for the same reason — it slurps every scenario in `data/src/partner_graph/output/scenarios/` to compile rules and dies fast on any parse error.

**Real root cause for the day's outage:** seven unresolved git merge markers in two scenario files. We resolved them (HEAD wins on every conflict — it has FAA-grounded compound segments and AIM-correct phraseology), and rolled in a `skip_invalid=True` knob on the loader so a single malformed scenario file can never again take the whole flight pipeline offline. fmm-daemon now boots with **182 rules** across all 8 scenarios.

**The smell that prompted this proposal:** the recorder file at `data/flights/<flight>/intents/chain-9442c5203fb2.json` had **1,373 identical JSONL records** for the same chain_id, same transcript, same intent. That is not normal at-least-once delivery behaviour. That is a hot loop.

Same chain. Same transcript. Same intent. 1,373 times in roughly three minutes — about 7 records per second. The FMM was unreachable, the JS handler kept throwing, NATS kept redelivering, and the recorder kept appending. Nobody acked. Nobody capped. Nobody slept.

props.label: ROOT-CAUSE TRIAD
props.title: Three bugs, one pathology
props.subtitle: Each is benign on its own. Together they form an amplifier with positive feedback.

props.content:
Think of this as three rings of a bullseye. **Bug 1** is the recorder mistaking JSONL for JSON — it turns one event into one accreting line. **Bug 2** is JetStream nak-without-backoff — it turns one transient downstream failure into an unbounded retry loop. **Bug 3** is the intent handler doing FMM forwarding inline — it converts a downstream outage into a callback exception that triggers Bug 2.

Fix any one ring and the storm caps at a manageable rate. Fix all three and the system gets honest at-least-once semantics with bounded blast radius.

props.label: BUG #1 · PERSISTENCE
props.title: Lineage recorder appends JSONL into a `.json` file
props.subtitle: The contract says single-record JSON. The implementation writes append-mode JSONL. The storm exploits the gap.

props.title: Where
props.content: `modules/persistence/recorder.py` · `record_lineage_artifact` · line 378. Path is `<flight_dir>/<subdir>/<chain_id>.json` (singular), opened in mode `"a"`. The docstring above says "Write a lineage-aware artifact … as JSON." Either the contract is wrong or the implementation is.

filepath = target_dir / f"{data['chain_id']}.json"
with filepath.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
    fh.write(record + "\n")

self._artifact_counts[artifact_type.value] += 1

props.label: BUG #2 · MESSAGING
props.title: JetStream `nak()` with no backoff and no delivery cap
props.subtitle: Any handler exception immediately re-queues the message. Forever.

props.title: Where
props.content: `modules/daemon/messaging.py` · `message_handler` inside `subscribe()` · lines 324–335. Bare `await msg.nak()` without `delay=...`. ConsumerConfig at line 377 omits `max_deliver`, so JetStream redelivers an unlimited number of times. Combined with handlers that can fail (Bug 3), this becomes a CPU-bound retry loop.

async def message_handler(msg: Any) -> None:
    try:
        envelope = EventEnvelope.from_bytes(msg.data)
        await callback(envelope)
        await msg.ack()
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(
            "nats_message_handler_error",
            topic=topic,
            error=str(e),
        )
        await msg.nak()

props.label: BUG #3 · INTENT FLOW
props.title: Intent handler is non-idempotent and forwards to FMM inline
props.subtitle: Every redelivery re-records, re-publishes, and re-attempts FMM. There is no chain_id memo and no transactional separation between local persistence and downstream forwarding.

props.title: Where
props.content: `modules/intent/coordinator.py` · `handle_parse_result` · line 483. `await self._fmm_gateway.send_proposal(proposal)` runs inside the JS callback. If FMM is down it raises, the whole handler raises, msg.nak fires, the storm starts. There is also no LRU of recently-processed `chain_id`s — the same audio gets re-parsed end-to-end on every redelivery.

props.label: AMPLIFIER LOOP
props.title: Why the three bugs together are worse than the sum
props.subtitle: Bug 2 generates redeliveries. Bug 3 keeps generating exceptions to nak on. Bug 1 etches every cycle into the disk.

props.title: Today — the storm in motion
props.content: ASR.final lands in JetStream. Intent daemon dequeues, parses, **writes lineage record (append)**, publishes intent.parsed, **forwards to FMM (raises — FMM dead)**. Handler raises. **`msg.nak()` fires with no delay**, JS redelivers immediately. Same chain, same transcript. **Loop**. ~7 cycles per second. Each cycle = +1 record file line. After 3 minutes: 1,373 lines for one chain. Every chain in flight is doing this.

props.title: After Tier 1 — honest at-least-once
props.content: ASR.final lands. Handler runs. If FMM is down, **`max_deliver=5`** caps the retries. Each retry is **delayed 2 seconds** by `nak(delay=2.0)`, not instant. The recorder writes in **`mode="w"`** so identical chain re-deliveries overwrite, not accrete. After 5 attempts JS terminal-acks and emits a structured failure event the dashboard can show. Worst case = 5 disk writes + 10 seconds of retry, then we surface the FMM outage to humans.

props.label: TIER 1 · SHIP NOW
props.title: Three small changes, two files, one PR
props.subtitle: Defensive only — caps blast radius without changing the intent handler control flow. Lowest possible risk for the demo window.

props.content: These three patches do not require changing the intent handler's logic, the message ordering, or the FMM gateway. They make the existing pieces honest about what they're already doing. **Goal: the next downed FMM is logged and survivable, not catastrophic.**

rows.c3: Same chain_id overwrites; the file becomes single-record JSON as the docstring already promises.
Poison messages get terminal-acked after 5 tries instead of looping forever.
Adds 2s backoff between deliveries. Storm rate drops from ~7/s to ~0.5/s, freeing CPU and giving the FMM time to recover.

rows.c2: New: assert second write with same chain_id replaces, not appends
rows.c3: Locks Bug #1 in place so it cannot regress.

rows.c2: New: mock broker, verify nak delay + ConsumerConfig carries max_deliver

props.label: DIFF SKETCH
props.title: Approximate patch shape
props.subtitle: These are the actual lines that change. Final tests + ruff cleanup add a few more, but ~30 LOC is the right ballpark.

props.content:
filepath = target_dir / f"{data['chain_id']}.json"
-        with filepath.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
-            fh.write(record + "\n")
+        # Lineage artifacts are one-record-per-chain by contract. Use
+        # write mode so JetStream redeliveries overwrite the same file
+        # instead of accreting JSONL lines (caused 1,373 dup records
+        # per chain on 2026-04-22 when fmm-daemon was down).
+        filepath.write_text(record + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

         self._artifact_counts[artifact_type.value] += 1

async def message_handler(msg: Any) -> None:
             try:
                 envelope = EventEnvelope.from_bytes(msg.data)
                 await callback(envelope)
                 await msg.ack()
             except Exception as e:
                 logger.error(
                     "nats_message_handler_error",
                     topic=topic,
                     error=str(e),
                 )
-                await msg.nak()
+                # Backoff so a downed downstream doesn't hot-loop
+                # the consumer. Pairs with max_deliver below.
+                await msg.nak(delay=2.0)

         ...

         sub = await self._js.subscribe(
             topic, stream=stream_name, durable=durable, queue=queue, cb=message_handler,
-            config=ConsumerConfig(deliver_policy=DeliverPolicy.NEW),
+            # Cap poison-message retries. After 5 attempts JetStream
+            # terminal-acks; surface the failure to operators instead
+            # of grinding indefinitely.
+            config=ConsumerConfig(deliver_policy=DeliverPolicy.NEW, max_deliver=5),
         )

props.label: TIER 2 · NEXT SESSION
props.title: Correctness pass for the intent flow
props.subtitle: Larger surface, deserves a design pass. Should land as its own PR with a dedicated handoff under `.lev/pm/handoffs/` so the rationale survives compaction.

props.content: Tier 1 caps the blast radius. Tier 2 makes the system **right** — separates "we processed the audio" from "FMM accepted the proposal," makes redelivery a no-op when the chain is already in the recorder, and turns FMM unreachable into a first-class observable status instead of an exception.

rows.c2: Ack the JS message after local-disk record + intent.parsed publish, BEFORE FMM forward
rows.c3: Local persistence is durable progress. FMM forwarding is a separate downstream concern that should retry via its own side-channel, not by re-parsing audio.

rows.c2: Bounded LRU cache of recently-processed `chain_id` (~5 min TTL)
rows.c3: Belt-and-suspenders idempotency against any future at-least-once edge case. Cheap; survives daemon restart by re-warming from the recorder.

rows.c2: Health-aware forwarding: emit structured `fmm_unreachable` event + simple circuit breaker
rows.c3: Turn an upstream-down condition into an observable state instead of a crash loop. The dashboard finally has something to render that says 'FMM is down.'
One bad scenario file can no longer kill the whole flight stack at boot.

rows.c2: Lift redelivery storms to dashboards: per-chain delivery counters + alert when > 3
rows.c3: We should learn about retry storms from a dashboard, not from a 1,373-line recorder file.

props.label: ROLLOUT
props.title: Suggested sequence
props.subtitle: Ordered by risk and demo proximity.

items.title: Tier 1 PR — ship the three defensive patches + tests
items.description: ~30 LOC across 2 files plus 2 new test files. Lint clean. Verifies fmm-daemon survives a full retry-storm scenario in CI without unbounded growth.

items.title: Loader hardening + merge conflict resolution
items.description: `skip_invalid=True` on `load_scenarios_dir`, merge-marker detection in `scenario_diff`, 7 conflicts resolved across 2 scenario files (HEAD wins on all). fmm-daemon boots with 182 rules across 8 scenarios.

items.title: Tier 2 design pass + handoff
items.description: Drop a `.lev/pm/handoffs/` note documenting the trio. Open a PR for 2a + 2b + 2c. Coordinate with the `flight-observability-blackbox` workstream on 2e — their dashboard is the natural place to surface delivery counters.

items.title: Recorder schema sweep
items.description: Audit every `.json` file written by the recorder for the same JSONL-vs-JSON contract drift. Bug 1 may not be unique.

props.label: OPEN QUESTIONS
props.title: Decisions you need to make
props.subtitle: These are the only judgement calls in the proposal. Everything else is mechanical.

props.title: Bug 1 fix — mode="w" or rename to .jsonl?
props.content:
**Recommended:** mode="w". Lineage records are by definition one-per-chain; redelivery should be a no-op, not a history. Renaming to `.jsonl` keeps a useful append history but every consumer (scenario_diff, dashboards, the gateway scan_flight_dir) would need to learn the new shape.

**Decision needed:** confirm mode="w" or push back.

props.title: max_deliver value — 5 or higher?
props.content:
**Recommended:** 5. Matches the typical NATS default for poison-pill protection and keeps the storm window under 10 seconds with a 2s nak delay. If FMM outages routinely last minutes, 10 may be safer.

**Decision needed:** pick a number you're comfortable with operationally.

props.title: Tier 2 timing — same week or next sprint?
props.content:
**Recommended:** next session, with a handoff dropped now. Tier 1 alone fully neutralises the demo-killing pathology; Tier 2 is a correctness improvement that benefits from a design pass and coordination with `flight-observability-blackbox`.

**Decision needed:** confirm cadence so I can size the handoff appropriately.

props.label: APPENDIX
props.title: Forensic detail — what the recorder actually contained
props.subtitle: For the curious. Skip if you trust the headline.

props.content: The audio the user spoke matched **e2e turn_index=2** (ATC → ownship weather delivery). With a working pipeline the system would have generated **"Roger, conditions VFR, November One Seven X-ray X-ray."** as the pilot's readback. Instead it generated 1,373 silent recorder lines and zero radio output.
